summary: The test suite covers core requirements well: version file parsing (plain text and JSON), conventional commit detection (feat, fix, breaking), version bumping logic, and changelog generation. The Pester unit tests are clear and well-organized by function. However, rigor is limited: missing edge cases include empty commits files, malformed commit messages, pre-release/build metadata versions, CHANGELOG append/replace idempotency, and missing file handling in Invoke-VersionBump. Integration testing via Run-ActTests.ps1 validates workflow structure and runs only 3 act scenarios. Design is adequate but inconsistent: some temp file cleanup lacks try-finally, tests could use parameterization to reduce duplication, and no shared fixtures for standard test data. The suite catches basic regressions but would miss many boundary conditions and real-world variations in commit messages.
